<result>
# MMS Fractal Planner - Project Specification

## Executive Summary

A revolutionary maintenance planning visualization system for railway operators, designed as a "Google Maps of Time" - enabling seamless navigation from 12-year strategic views down to daily operations through continuous zoom interactions.

---

## 1. Business Context

### 1.1 The Opportunity
This project responds to an RFQ from a major railway operator. After a suboptimal initial presentation, we must deliver an impressive demo that:
- Recovers client confidence
- Proves technical excellence through fluid, zero-latency interactions
- Addresses all RFQ requirements visually and functionally

### 1.2 Success Criteria
- **60 FPS** maintained during all interactions
- **Seamless zoom** from 12-year to 1-day views
- **Professional aesthetics** matching high-end planning tools
- **Zero perceived latency** on drag, zoom, and pan operations

---

## 2. Core Concepts

### 2.1 The Fractal Time Metaphor

Like Google Maps enables seamless zoom from continent to street level, MMS Fractal Planner enables:
- **Zoom out**: 12 years of strategic planning (heatmaps, capacity trends)
- **Zoom in**: Daily operations (Gantt charts, individual train blocks)
- **Continuous transition**: No page reloads, fluid semantic zoom
- **Bidirectional propagation**: Changes at any level affect all others

### 2.2 Semantic Zoom Levels

| Level | Temporal Scale | Default Visualization | Purpose |
|-------|---------------|----------------------|---------|
| L1 | 12 Years | Capacity Heatmap | Strategic CAPEX planning |
| L2 | 3-5 Years | Stacked Bar + Trends | Fleet lifecycle patterns |
| L3 | 1-2 Years | Combo Charts | Seasonality analysis |
| L4 | Quadrimester | Workload Equalizer | Capacity balancing |
| L5 | 1-3 Months | Weekly Bars | Resource planning |
| L6 | 2-4 Weeks | Simplified Gantt | Track occupancy |
| L7 | 1 Week | Detailed Gantt | Per-train scheduling |
| L8 | 1-3 Days | Capsules + Details | Execution view |

### 2.3 Visualization Selector

Each zoom level offers multiple visualization options (like Google Maps' Map/Satellite/Terrain):
- User can switch between available charts for current zoom
- Each level has a recommended default
- Smooth transitions between visualization types

---

## 3. Technical Architecture

### 3.1 Core Principles

| Aspect | Approach | Rationale |
|--------|----------|-----------|
| **Rendering** | PixiJS (WebGL) | GPU-accelerated, handles 50K+ objects |
| **Data** | Struct of Arrays (SoA) | Cache-friendly, SIMD-compatible |
| **State** | Zustand | Minimal overhead, no proxy magic |
| **Scrolling** | Virtual (math-based) | No DOM overhead, infinite canvas |
| **Animation** | requestAnimationFrame | Synced with display refresh |

### 3.2 Forbidden Patterns

| Pattern | Why Forbidden |
|---------|---------------|
| DOM-based scrolling | Jank at scale, layout thrashing |
| Array of Objects | Poor cache locality for large datasets |
| Redux/Context for hot paths | Overhead on high-frequency updates |
| SVG/DOM for data viz | Cannot handle 50K+ elements |
| setInterval for animation | Not synced with refresh rate |

### 3.3 Directory Structure

```
src/
├── engine/           # Core logic, SoA stores, physics
│   ├── stores/       # Zustand stores (simulation, UI state)
│   ├── soa/          # Struct of Arrays implementations
│   └── collision/    # Interval tree, constraint detection
├── render/           # PixiJS rendering layer
│   ├── layers/       # Visual layers (blocks, grid, overlays)
│   ├── shaders/      # Custom WebGL shaders
│   └── viewport/     # Zoom, pan, virtual scroll
├── ui/               # React components (overlays only)
│   ├── panels/       # Sidebar, constraint deck
│   ├── controls/     # Toolbar, zoom controls
│   └── overlays/     # Modal dialogs, tooltips
└── services/         # API layer, mock data
```

---

## 4. Data Model

### 4.1 Fleet Structure

```typescript
interface Train {
  id: string;
  series: string;        // e.g., "S-103", "S-112"
  trainNumber: string;   // e.g., "03", "07"
  homeBase: string;      // Workshop assignment
}

interface MaintenanceBlock {
  id: string;
  trainId: string;
  type: MaintenanceType;
  startDate: number;     // Unix timestamp
  endDate: number;
  trackId: string;
  status: BlockStatus;
}
```

### 4.2 Maintenance Types

| Type | Duration | Frequency | Color |
|------|----------|-----------|-------|
| Daily Inspection | 2-4h | Daily | Blue |
| Weekly Service | 4-8h | Weekly | Green |
| Monthly Check | 1-2 days | Monthly | Yellow |
| Quarterly Overhaul | 3-5 days | Quarterly | Orange |
| Annual Revision | 2-4 weeks | Annual | Red |
| GR (Major Overhaul) | 4-8 weeks | 6-8 years | Purple |

### 4.3 Struct of Arrays (SoA) Format

```typescript
interface BlockSoA {
  // Parallel arrays for cache efficiency
  ids: string[];
  trainIds: Uint32Array;      // Index references
  startDates: Float64Array;   // Unix timestamps
  endDates: Float64Array;
  trackIds: Uint16Array;
  types: Uint8Array;          // Enum index
  statuses: Uint8Array;
  
  // Computed for rendering
  xPositions: Float32Array;
  widths: Float32Array;
  yPositions: Float32Array;
}
```

---

## 5. Rendering Pipeline

### 5.1 Layer Stack

```
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│  UI Overlay (React/DOM)         │  ← Tooltips, dialogs
├─────────────────────────────────┤
│  Interaction Layer (PixiJS)     │  ← Drag handles, selection
├─────────────────────────────────┤
│  Block Layer (PixiJS)           │  ← Maintenance blocks
├─────────────────────────────────┤
│  Grid Layer (PixiJS)            │  ← Time grid, track lanes
├─────────────────────────────────┤
│  Background (WebGL)             │  ← Gradient, patterns
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 5.2 Virtual Viewport

```typescript
interface Viewport {
  // Temporal range
  startTime: number;    // Visible start (Unix ms)
  endTime: number;      // Visible end
  
  // Zoom level
  msPerPixel: number;   // Temporal density
  semanticLevel: 1-8;   // Current zoom level
  
  // Vertical scroll
  firstVisibleTrack: number;
  visibleTrackCount: number;
}
```

### 5.3 Culling Strategy

Only render blocks that intersect the viewport:
1. Use Interval Tree for temporal queries
2. Filter by visible track range
3. Apply Level of Detail (LoD) based on zoom

---

## 6. Interaction Model

### 6.1 Navigation

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| Scroll wheel | Zoom in/out (centered on cursor) |
| Ctrl + Scroll | Horizontal pan |
| Click + Drag (background) | Pan viewport |
| Pinch gesture | Zoom (touch devices) |

### 6.2 Block Manipulation

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| Click block | Select |
| Shift + Click | Multi-select |
| Drag block | Move (constrained) |
| Drag edge | Resize duration |
| Double-click | Open detail panel |

### 6.3 Constraint System

Blocks snap to valid positions based on:
- Track availability
- Minimum intervals between maintenance
- Workshop capacity
- Resource availability

---

## 7. State Management

### 7.1 Store Architecture

```typescript
// Transient state (high-frequency updates)
useViewportStore     // Zoom, pan, scroll position
useDragStore         // Active drag operation
useHoverStore        // Hover target

// Application state (lower frequency)
useSimulationStore   // Block data (SoA), constraints
useSelectionStore    // Selected block IDs
useUIStore           // Panel visibility, theme
```

### 7.2 Update Flow

```
User Input → Gesture Handler → Store Update → 
  → Viewport recalc → Culling → Render
```

All within single rAF frame when possible.

---

## 8. Performance Budgets

| Metric | Budget | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Frame time | <16ms | Performance.now() delta |
| Input latency | <8ms | Input to first paint |
| Initial load | <3s | DOMContentLoaded to interactive |
| Memory | <500MB | Chrome DevTools heap |
| Bundle size | <1MB | gzipped |

---

## 9. Demo Requirements

### 9.1 Must-Have for Demo

1. **Seamless 8-level zoom** with visual transitions
2. **Drag-and-drop** block rescheduling
3. **Constraint visualization** (conflicts highlighted)
4. **Time navigation** (jump to date, today button)
5. **Train filtering** (by series, status)
6. **Baseline vs Scenario** toggle

### 9.2 Visual Polish

- Professional dark theme (railway industry standard)
- Smooth animations (60fps)
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Responsive to window resize
- Keyboard navigation support

---

## 10. Development Phases

### Phase 0: Foundation
- Project setup (Vite, TypeScript, PixiJS)
- Basic rendering pipeline
- Viewport math utilities

### Phase 1: Core Engine
- SoA data structures
- Virtual scrolling
- Basic block rendering

### Phase 2: Interactions
- Zoom/pan navigation
- Block selection
- Drag-and-drop

### Phase 3: Visualizations
- Multi-level rendering
- Heatmaps, Gantt charts
- Smooth transitions

### Phase 4: Polish
- Constraint system
- UI panels
- Performance optimization

---

## Appendix: Quick Reference

### Color Palette

```css
--bg-primary: #0a0e17;
--bg-secondary: #141923;
--accent-blue: #3b82f6;
--accent-green: #22c55e;
--accent-orange: #f59e0b;
--accent-red: #ef4444;
--text-primary: #f1f5f9;
--text-secondary: #94a3b8;
```

### Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/engine/stores/simulation.ts` | Main data store |
| `src/render/viewport/Viewport.ts` | Zoom/pan logic |
| `src/render/layers/BlockLayer.ts` | Block rendering |
| `src/engine/soa/BlockSoA.ts` | Data structure |
</result>

Now I have a clear picture of the project state. Let me summarize my findings:

---

## Codebase Analysis Summary

### Current State

This is a **brand new project** at the very beginning of development. The repository contains only:

1. **CLAUDE.md** - AI agent instructions for development
2. **PROJECT.md** - Complete technical specification (SSOT)
3. **features.json** - Task tracking with 5 phases, all tasks are `pending`
4. **README.md** - Minimal project description

**No source code exists yet.** The `src/`, `docs/`, and other directories mentioned in the specification have not been created.

### Project Overview

**MMS Fractal Planner** is a railway maintenance scheduling visualization system - described as "Google Maps of Time". Key characteristics:

- **8 semantic zoom levels**: From 12-year strategic view down to 1-3 day execution view
- **WebGL rendering**: PixiJS v8 for GPU-accelerated performance (target: 50K+ blocks at 60fps)
- **Struct of Arrays (SoA)**: TypedArrays for cache-efficient data structures
- **Virtual scrolling**: Math-based, no DOM ghost elements
- **Zustand state management**: For both transient and application state

### Next Steps Required

According to `features.json`, the immediate tasks are **Phase 0: Foundation**:

| Task ID | Name | Priority |
|---------|------|----------|
| 0.1 | Project Setup | Critical |
| 0.2 | Directory Structure | Critical |
| 0.3 | Basic Rendering Pipeline | Critical |
| 0.4 | Viewport Math Utilities | High |

### Critical Files for Implementation

Since no code exists yet, these are the files that need to be **created first**:

- **/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/m2/package.json** - "Project dependencies and scripts (Vite, React, TypeScript, PixiJS v8, Zustand)"
- **/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/m2/vite.config.ts** - "Build configuration for the application"
- **/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/m2/tsconfig.json** - "TypeScript strict mode configuration"
- **/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/m2/src/main.tsx** - "Application entry point"
- **/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/m2/src/render/viewport/Viewport.ts** - "Core viewport transformation logic (timeToX, xToTime, etc.)"

### Architecture Constraints to Follow

From CLAUDE.md and PROJECT.md:

| Allowed | Forbidden |
|---------|-----------|
| Virtual Scroller (Math-based) | Native DOM Scrolling / Ghost Divs |
| TypedArrays, SoA, Maps | Array of Objects (for massive data) |
| Zustand | Redux, Context API (for high-freq updates) |
| PixiJS (WebGL) | DOM Elements (for >100 items), SVG |
| requestAnimationFrame | setInterval, setTimeout (for animation) |
